Content Packs

Content packs are JSON files that seed a fresh Outpost install with collections, items, menus, globals, and folders. They power the setup wizard's "starter content" step.

Overview

Content packs live in the content-packs/ directory alongside the core PHP files. A manifest file (packs.json) lists available packs with metadata and theme compatibility. Individual pack files are named {theme}-{pack}.json.

outpost/
  content-packs/
    packs.json                  # Manifest
    personal-blog.json          # Blog pack for Personal theme
    personal-portfolio.json     # Portfolio pack for Personal theme
    starter-blog.json           # Blog pack for Starter theme
    starter-business.json       # Business pack for Starter theme
    starter-portfolio.json      # Portfolio pack for Starter theme
    skeleton-blog.json          # Blog pack for Skeleton theme
    skeleton-business.json      # Business pack for Skeleton theme
    skeleton-portfolio.json     # Portfolio pack for Skeleton theme

Manifest: packs.json

The manifest is a JSON array of pack definitions:

[
  {
    "id": "blog",
    "name": "Blog",
    "description": "Personal blog with sample posts and categories.",
    "icon": "pencil",
    "themes": ["personal", "starter", "skeleton"]
  },
  {
    "id": "portfolio",
    "name": "Portfolio",
    "description": "Portfolio with projects, about page, and contact.",
    "icon": "grid",
    "themes": ["personal", "starter", "skeleton"]
  },
  {
    "id": "business",
    "name": "Business",
    "description": "Business site with services, team, and contact form.",
    "icon": "briefcase",
    "themes": ["starter", "skeleton"]
  },
  {
    "id": "blank",
    "name": "Start from scratch",
    "description": "Just the theme — no sample content.",
    "icon": "plus",
    "themes": ["personal", "starter", "skeleton"]
  }
]
Field Type Description
id string Unique pack identifier, used as the second part of the pack filename
name string Display name shown in the wizard
description string Short description shown below the name
icon string Icon key (pencil, grid, briefcase, plus) for the wizard UI
themes string[] Theme slugs this pack is compatible with
Note — The blank pack has no corresponding JSON file. When selected, the wizard activates the theme without seeding any content.

Pack File Format

Each pack file is a JSON object with optional top-level keys:

{
  "settings": {
    "site_name": "My Blog"
  },
  "globals": [
    { "field_name": "site_tagline", "type": "text", "content": "Welcome to my blog" }
  ],
  "collections": [
    {
      "name": "Posts",
      "slug": "post",
      "schema": [ ... ],
      "url_pattern": "/post/{slug}",
      "items": [
        {
          "title": "Hello World",
          "slug": "hello-world",
          "status": "published",
          "data": { "excerpt": "My first post", "body": "<p>Hello!</p>" }
        }
      ],
      "folders": [
        {
          "name": "Categories",
          "slug": "categories",
          "labels": ["News", "Tutorials", "Personal"]
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "menus": [
    {
      "slug": "main",
      "name": "Main Menu",
      "items": [
        { "label": "Home", "url": "/" },
        { "label": "Blog", "url": "/blog" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

Settings

Key-value pairs inserted into the settings table via INSERT OR REPLACE.

Globals

Array of global field definitions. The pack ensures the __global__ page exists before inserting fields.

FieldTypeDescription
field_namestringThe global field name (e.g. site_tagline)
typestringField type: text, richtext, image, textarea, etc.
contentstringInitial value

Collections

Array of collection definitions, each with optional nested items and folders.

Menus

Array of menu definitions with nested items. Each item has label and url fields.

How Packs Are Applied

The POST setup/apply endpoint processes everything in a single SQLite transaction:

  1. Update site_name in settings
  2. Activate the selected theme (reuses the existing theme activation logic)
  3. Load the pack JSON file ({theme}-{pack}.json)
  4. Insert globals (ensuring __global__ page exists)
  5. Insert collections with schema, items, folders, and labels
  6. Insert menus with items
  7. Set setup_completed = '1'
  8. Clear the template cache

If any step fails, the entire transaction is rolled back and no partial content is left behind.

Creating Custom Packs

To add a custom content pack:

  1. Create a JSON file following the pack format (e.g. starter-agency.json)
  2. Add an entry to packs.json with the matching id and theme compatibility
  3. The pack will appear in the setup wizard for compatible themes
Tip — Content packs are only used during initial setup. They don't affect existing content, and changing a pack file after setup has no effect on the site.
